description: The coding-task lifecycle stabilization work is tracked as a new harness task so future sessions can resume from the real Telegram/tmux failure evidence instead of the already-completed `/coding help` follow-up.
steps: Open PLAN.json and verify it now tracks the coding-task lifecycle stabilization task instead of the prior completed `/coding help` harness.

Open PROGRESS.md and verify the reboot entry records the real stuck-task evidence from task ids `681ee161` and `1be6a750`, including the fact that progress kept updating while status stayed `starting`.
Confirm the baseline explicitly keeps the unrelated Matrix optional dependency failure (`nio` missing during top-level pytest collection) out of scope for this task.

description: A coding task promoted by live tmux or log activity no longer stays stuck in `starting`; effective worker activity advances it into `running` and keeps the latest progress summary fresh.
steps: Create a task in `starting` with real non-shell pane output such as command execution or agent commentary and verify refresh changes it to `running`.
Verify shell prompts, startup-only diagnostics, and waiting-for-user text do not count as effective running activity.
Verify the persisted task keeps an updated `last_progress_summary` and timestamp as the worker continues emitting useful activity.

description: Worker-exit triage distinguishes between hard failure, explicit harness completion, and exit-review wait states instead of always failing missing tmux sessions.
steps: When the tmux worker disappears and the target repo harness is fully complete, verify the task transitions to `completed`.

When the tmux worker disappears with only strong completion evidence but no explicit harness completion, verify the task transitions to `waiting_user` with an exit-review explanation instead of `failed`.
When the tmux worker disappears without strong completion evidence, verify the task transitions to `failed` with the last useful progress or recovery hint.

description: Exit-review wait states are visible for inspection and resume, but they no longer block new coding-task starts as if a worker were still actively running.
steps: Create a `waiting_user` task tagged as worker-exit review and verify workspace blocking ignores it for new task creation.
Verify the same exit-review task still appears in visible task lists and remains selectable for `/coding status` and `/coding resume`.
Verify active harness-conflict waits still keep their previous blocking behavior.

description: Completion, failure, and exit-review reports expose repo, goal, branch, recent commit, latest note or last meaningful progress, and explicit next actions across Telegram and CLI status surfaces.
steps: Verify `nanobot coding-task status <task_id>` prints richer completed, failed, and exit-review summaries without losing existing task metadata fields.
Verify Telegram `/coding status` for an exit-review task shows the latest meaningful progress plus clear next actions for resume versus passive review.
Verify notifier output for `completed`, `failed`, and exit-review `waiting_user` states reuses the same improved report content.

description: Startup recovery and steady-state polling use the same lifecycle rules, so restart recovery no longer mis-completes live tasks or mis-fails reviewable tasks.
steps: Recover a task whose tmux session still exists and verify it stays observable as active instead of flipping to `completed` just because the repo plan is already green.
Recover a missing-session task with explicit harness completion and verify it finishes as `completed`.
Recover a missing-session task with only strong completion evidence and verify it enters the exit-review wait state rather than `failed`.

description: Focused coding-task tests cover running promotion, exit-review triage, reporting, recovery, and non-blocking review waits without reopening old `/coding` UX scope.
steps: Run `.venv/bin/pytest tests/coding_tasks -q` and verify the updated suite covers `starting -> running`, live-session non-completion, missing-session `completed/failed/waiting_user`, and richer report content.
Verify policy tests cover exit-review waits remaining visible but not workspace-blocking.
Confirm `.venv/bin/python -m compileall` still succeeds for the touched coding-task modules and tests.

description: CLI and gateway-focused regressions are aligned with the current implementation so lifecycle stabilization can be validated end-to-end from the active runtime path.
steps: Run `.venv/bin/pytest tests/agent/test_coding_task_routing.py tests/channels/test_telegram_channel.py tests/cli/test_commands.py -q` and verify the suite passes after updating outdated gateway and launch-result test doubles.
Verify gateway/CLI tests cover the current `publish_outbound` expectation, the full `CodexLaunchResult` shape, and the fixed serve-command assertions.
Confirm the focused gateway and CLI verification still leaves the unrelated Matrix optional dependency gap explicitly out of scope.

description: Local tmux/runtime verification proves the stabilized lifecycle with a workspace-scoped coding-task runtime and real tmux sessions, even without a live Telegram bot token.
steps: Launch a workspace-scoped task through the real tmux-backed worker path and verify it advances from `starting` to `running` when the fake worker emits meaningful activity.

Verify a real missing-session task with only strong completion evidence transitions to the exit-review wait state, and a real missing-session task with a fully completed harness transitions to `completed`.
Verify the resulting `tasks.json`, run log, and `nanobot coding-task status` output agree on the final lifecycle state.
